name: Okta Repeated Account Lock Events (24 hours)
description: Detects repeated Okta account lock events that can indicate brute-force, password-spray, or credential stuffing activity.
query: SELECT COALESCE(l.target_id, l.actor_id, l.uuid) AS id, COALESCE(target_user.login, target_user.email, actor_user.login, actor_user.email, l.target_id, l.actor_id) AS account_name, COUNT(*) AS lock_events, MAX(l.published) AS last_locked_at FROM okta_system_logs l LEFT JOIN okta_users target_user ON target_user.id = l.target_id LEFT JOIN okta_users actor_user ON actor_user.id = l.actor_id WHERE LOWER(COALESCE(l.event_type, '')) IN ('user.account.lock', 'user.account.lock.limit') AND l.published >= CURRENT_TIMESTAMP() - INTERVAL '24 hours' GROUP BY COALESCE(l.target_id, l.actor_id, l.uuid), COALESCE(target_user.login, target_user.email, actor_user.login, actor_user.email, l.target_id, l.actor_id) HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
remediation: Investigate lock spikes, verify whether attacks are in progress, and enforce adaptive controls (MFA, network/rate limiting, and account protection policies).
